description: The Rathkeale Rovers are a criminal group based in Ireland but operating internationally, composed of members of Ireland's Travelling Community. They engage in fraud, money laundering, drug smuggling, and art theft, notably stealing rhino horns and Chinese antiquities from museums. The group originated with Simon Quilligan and was later led by the O'Brien family. Europol and US authorities have investigated them, leading to multiple arrests.

country_links.evidence_quote: In October 2013, the New South Wales Fair Trading department issued a warning that the gang was operating on the Australian eastern seaboard.
country_links.context: The Rathkeale Rovers were operating on the Australian eastern seaboard.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 13 April 2012, the gang broke into Cambridge University's Fitzwilliam Museum... After the sentencing of the 14, a police spokesman said that there were other members of the Rathkeale Rovers still at large in the UK.
country_links.context: The Rathkeale Rovers carried out museum thefts in the UK and had members still at large there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2013, the United States arrested the Rathkeale Rovers on US soil.
country_links.context: The Rathkeale Rovers were arrested on US soil for smuggling rhinoceros horns.
